intro:
I couldn't find any information on "Crocodeilanthe domingensis". However, I found information on "Croton lechleri" which is also known as "Dragons Blood". Here's a paragraph about it:

Croton lechleri, commonly known as Dragon's Blood, is a plant species that belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae. The resin of this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, providing health benefits such as wound healing, reducing inflammation, and treating digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Croton lechleri include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phytosterols, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Croton lechleri include tinctures, capsules, and topical creams, often used to treat minor wounds, skin irritations, and digestive problems.
